content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print the grade of a student based on the marks he/she has obtained.

**Grading Rules**


Grade A → Marks > 90
Grade B → Marks > 70
Grade C → Marks ≥ 40
Grade F → Marks < 40
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer M (0 ≤ M ≤ 100) representing the marks of a student.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single character – A, B, C or F – on its own line representing the grade corresponding to the given marks.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Marks 40 is not > 70 nor > 90 but >= 40, so grade is C.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times reading the marks M. Use a chain of if‑else statements: if M>90 output 'A'; else if M>70 output 'B'; else if M>=40 output 'C'; else output 'F'. Print each grade on a new line. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.
